label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The user wants to know what we can infer about garage sales from the given passage. First, I need to look at the options provided: A, B, C, D. The passage mentions that garage sales are usually held in spring and fall when the weather is good. So option A says "only in spring," but the text says "usually... in spring and fall," which means it's not only spring. So A is incorrect. Option B states that the goods are "totally useless." The passage says the homeowner wants to clear out items that are no longer useful to them, but the items are often in good condition and can be great buys. So B is incorrect because they're described as useful and in good condition. Option C says garage sales are popular with Americans. The text says they've "become very much a part of American culture," which definitely implies popularity. So C seems correct. Option D claims all goods are sold
